repo_url.description: GitHub URL of the repository containing the finding.

file.description: Path of the file (relative to the repo root) where the secret was found.

permalink.description: Direct GitHub URL to the file:line where the secret appears (clone mode uses commit SHA; code_search uses default branch).

secret_name.description: Short label of the rule that fired (e.g. 'razorpay-key-secret').

paired.description: 'yes' if the secret was paired with its corresponding ID in the same file.

paired_with.description: When paired='yes', contains {name, value, line} of the paired component. Null otherwise.

platform.description: Service or label the scan targeted (e.g. 'razorpay', 'keyword:anthropic', 'regex:custom').

scan_method.description: Which engine produced the finding: 'clone' = git history scan via gitleaks; 'code_search' = GitHub Code Search API.

commit_sha.description: Commit SHA where the secret was found (clone mode) or blob SHA (code_search mode).

is_test_key.description: True if the secret matches a public-by-design test/sandbox pattern.

author_name.description: Commit author name (clone mode only; empty for code_search).

author_email.description: Commit author email (clone mode only; empty for code_search).

commit_date.description: ISO timestamp of the commit (clone mode only; empty for code_search).

branch_ref.description: Which git ref the leak was reached from. Examples: 'history' (any reachable ref), 'dangling' (unreachable commit, found via git fsck). Helps determine recovery effort.

is_dangling.description: True if the commit holding the leak is unreachable from any branch — found via git fsck --dangling. Indicates the developer tried to delete the leak via rebase but didn't garbage-collect.

overview.title: Findings
overview.description: All leaked secrets across scanned repos.
